findings.finding: The learner-visible rule says Q(t)=Q0 a^t grows when a>1 and Q(t)=Q0 e^(kt) grows when k>0, but this is false without the condition Q0>0: Q0=0 gives a constant function and Q0<0 reverses the ordinary increasing/decreasing behavior. claims.json#claim-exponential-parameterizations includes the positive-initial-value restriction, but the central explanation omits it.
findings.resolution: State Q0>0 explicitly beside both growth/decay rules and preserve the positive-base conditions. Clarify that the stated interpretation applies to positive modeled quantities; do not leave the essential boundary only in the claim ledger.

findings.finding: The orientation starting-check feedback teaches the material claim that logarithms split products rather than sums, but claim-log-laws omits scene-orientation from sceneIds and the same scene's source note omits claim-log-laws. This leaves a central objective-level rule without the required same-scene claim-to-source trace.
findings.resolution: Add scene-orientation to claim-log-laws.sceneIds and add claim-log-laws to the source note in content/010-orientation.md#check-starting-prerequisites, retaining source-nist-dlmf-log as its supporting source.

findings.finding: The prompt asks when the model first exceeds 10,000, but t=log2(40)≈5.32 is the equality threshold, not an exceeding time. The later completed-cycle answer of 6 is correct, yet the transition can conflate equality with a strict inequality.
findings.resolution: Label 5.32 as the equality threshold. State that continuous-model values exceed 10,000 for t>5.32, while the first completed integer cycle is 6.

findings.finding: The locator attributes exponential definitions and general powers to DLMF §4.2(i)-(ii), although those subsections cover the logarithm and logarithms to a general base; the exponential function and powers are §4.2(iii)-(iv). The accessed edition is also identifiable more exactly as DLMF version 1.2.7, released 2026-06-15.
findings.resolution: Correct the locator to include §4.2(iii)-(iv) for exponential functions and powers, and record the exact DLMF edition or release identifier checked.

findings.finding: Independent recalculation confirms the keyed threshold time 13.412 h and half-life 5.776 h, including the signs, positive dimensionless ratios, and reverse-substitution checks. The other numerical examples and assessment keys checked were also arithmetically correct within their stated rounding.

limitations: The review was limited to academic correctness at the declared premed quantitative-foundations level; the biomedical contexts were assessed only as explicitly fictional mathematical models and not as empirical pharmacology, microbiology, or clinical claims.

Per the assigned scope restriction, site/data/premed-terminology.json and the site graph data were not inspected. Cross-lesson terminology continuity and exact graph-node existence therefore remain unverified; the broader outcome scope was checked against course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md#QRS-102.

Per the assigned scope restriction, site schemas, quorum files, scripts, workflows, and other lesson packs were not read, and repository validation was not run. The frozen candidate pack itself had no working-tree drift.
Both declared sources were accessible and had checked-no-agent-restriction-found status. Their relevant public NIST sections were opened directly; no human-only evidence was encountered.
